data.narrative:
Overland AI is a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by Byron Boots, Greg Okopal, and Stephanie Bonk, having raised $40M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 10 comparables across public and private markets: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Scale AI, Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, and Anduril Industries.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression, using the $40M raised as a proxy base and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yields $80M. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same-stage comparables—Scale AI ($29.0B), Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M)—produces a median valuation of $400M, with the peer range spanning $150M to $29.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in defense-tech maturity and scope.

Triangulating these two methods supports a valuation range of $68M (low) to $460M (high), with a median estimate of $240M. The stage-progression method anchors conservatively at the lower end, while peer-median reflects the upper bound of activity in this sector. Key uncertainties include the relevance of Scale AI's $29B valuation as a true comparable given its distinct business model, and the extent to which Overland AI's specific technology and addressable market align with the broader peer cohort. A narrowed range of $150M–$300M may be more defensible pending clarification of product-market fit and revenue trajectory.
